summary: C++ template instantiation error during build of pandas SIMD moments module. An explicit instantiation for xsimd::neon64 cannot find a matching template definition, which is only defined for xsimd::neon.
root_cause: In pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp, line 255 contains an explicit template instantiation for accumulate_moments_simd::operator()<xsimd::neon64>, but the actual template definition at line 182 is only defined for xsimd::neon (without neon64). The template parameter type mismatch (neon64 vs neon) causes the compiler to reject the explicit instantiation.

evidence.quote: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Compiler directly states the explicit instantiation cannot resolve to any matching template definition
Line 255 shows the explicit instantiation attempting to instantiate for xsimd::neon64

evidence.quote: could not match 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int) noexcept' against 'Moments (xsimd::neon, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int)'
evidence.reason: Shows the specific mismatch: the explicit instantiation expects neon64 but the template is for neon, and noexcept specifications differ
Compilation failed for the NEON SIMD moments instantiation object file during ninja build

suggested_fix: Update the explicit template instantiation in pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp line 255 to use xsimd::neon instead of xsimd::neon64, or update the template definition at line 182 to support neon64 specialization. Ensure the noexcept specifier is consistent between the declaration and definition.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
